title: Funding decision policy
text: The graduate committee is responsible for allocating departmental fellowships to graduate students each academic year. This document outlines the criteria and process used to make funding decisions.

title: Funding decision policy
text:
Eligibility
To be considered for a departmental fellowship, students must:

Be enrolled full-time in a graduate degree program (M.S. or Ph.D.)
Be in good academic standing (GPA ≥ 3.0)
Be making satisfactory progress toward their degree, as determined by their advisor and the graduate committee
Not exceed the standard funding period (X years for M.S., X years for Ph.D.)

title: Funding decision policy
text:
Allocation criteria
The graduate committee uses the following criteria when allocating fellowships. These are considered holistically — no single factor is determinative.

Year in program: Priority is given to students in earlier years of their program, subject to satisfactory progress requirements.
Academic and research performance: Evidence of progress includes completed coursework, publications, conference presentations, and advisor evaluations.
Advisor co-funding: Students whose advisors contribute partial funding (e.g., from grants) may receive higher priority for departmental support to complete their package.
Fellowship eligibility: Students who meet criteria for external fellowships may be nominated or given bridge funding while applications are pending.

title: Funding decision policy
text:
Decision process

At the start of each funding cycle, faculty advisors submit funding requests and any co-funding commitments for their students.
The graduate committee reviews all requests alongside available departmental funds.
Fellowship offers are made in priority order based on the criteria above.
Students are notified of their funding status by [deadline].

title: Funding decision policy
text:
Appeals
Students who believe their funding decision was made in error may submit a written appeal to the graduate committee chair within 30 days of notification. Appeals should include any relevant information not previously considered.

title: Funding decision policy
text:
Questions
Questions about the funding process should be directed to the graduate committee chair or the department’s graduate coordinator.

title: Offer Letter Request
text:
Use the form below (or the button) to submit an offer letter request for a new or existing graduate student. The form will notify the Graduate Program Coordinator and relevant staff once submitted.
Submit Offer Letter Request

title: Offer Letter Request
text:
What to Prepare
Before filling out the form, have the following information ready:

Student name — full legal name for new admits, or select from the dropdown for existing students
Degree program — MS or PhD
Funding sources and cost objects — one per line, separated by |
Annual salary / stipend amount
Funding breakdown by cost object
Funding start and end dates
Years of secured funding
Funding plan beyond the secured period (new students only, if fewer than 4 years secured)

title: Offer Letter Request
text:
After Submission
Once the form is submitted:

An email summary is sent to the Graduate Program Coordinator and staff.
The offer letter is generated and shared with you.

For questions, contact the Graduate Committee Chair at tmieno2@nebraska.edu.
